item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So first I wanna say I really liked this and I am upvoting… but there are so many unanswered questions without obvious clues as to their answers.

I can’t help but feel there should be more here, and addressing of the memory storage… the foundation has access to the codes the sub is using but instead of trying to respond in kind they try to force the door open… seems like a missed opportunity to examine the contents of the memory storage.

Overall I liked it, but I just feel unsatisfied by the ending. Feels like 2/3 of a well fleshed our article.

Though the dialogue was good, that's all I got from the article. I don't understand why the first part couldn't be woven into the 2nd part to make it shorter, and the reveal at the end did not do much for me. It was just another piece of information that had no stake inside the story until the end. Yeah, I was wondering then why it was full, but how we got that information was not satisfying at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just thought I should point out a few typos since they were bugging me:

RESTRICTED TO LEVEL 4 PERSONEL ONLY. PLEASE INPUT LEVEL 4 CREDENTIALS TO CONINUE.

And there's one in the image, where you missed the i in 'identifiers'. Also wondering whether you mean 'wanning' or 'waning'?

Asides from this, I really like this article. The ending left me with that good old 'oh shit' feeling, and I'd love to see more of the characters elsewhere. Good job!

Definite +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, thank you so much - you comment means so much to me. I'm really glad you enjoyed it!

If it's any consolation, I do certainly intend on taking characters and threads from this work forward - so with some luck there will be more to come!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the concept behind this article. I thought it was incredibly well-written. It might have been a small or even unintentional detail, but I adore that, in the second iteration of the article, Agent Murphy took on the role of a protagonist in a mystery novel. In a beautiful shift in tone and style, the article became this raw, character-driven, authentic investigation into a fascinating concept.

My only complaint is that the story ended waaaay too early. The entire article felt like the exposition of a mystery thriller, especially considering Dr. Minen was never found. I'm assuming you're saving that character for another article, considering Dr. Werner was from a previous article of yours, but still. There are so many ways to expand on this SCP, and I wish you had written even just a little bit more about it.

Easy +1 from me.

item.title: Re: --OMG I CRITTED THIS-- Very good, me like
item.content:encoded:
No need to thank me! Honestly, without you and the other reviewers helping me rewrite the dialogue I don't think this would have stayed up!

I'm glad to hear you liked this final version!

item.title: --OMG I CRITTED THIS-- Very good, me like
item.content:encoded:
AYYYY, LOOK WHO MADE THE HONOR LIST YEEEE

Wow, I'm glad this main the mainsite, it was interesting read through, and I'm comfortable giving this a +1.

Also, thx for putting me on the honor list, it mean's a lot!

~Yellow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Audibly "oh-no"'d when I read the last interview.
+1

item.title: SCP-5194
item.content:encoded:
The Moon reflects only part of the information back:

some is absorbed in its shadows or lost in its craters.

- Katie Paterson, Earth-Moon-Earth.

A huge thank-you to all those who reviewed this in its various stages of drafts, especially: YellowISlol, Comrade Waldo, PlaguePJP, and Dr John Silver.
